key_insight: Detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings form a thermally stabilized multiphase structure that resists oxidation at 450�b0C while hardening over time.
engineering_relevance: These coatings offer a protective barrier for TC4 titanium alloys in high-temperature service, improving oxidation resistance and retaining mechanical integrity during long-term exposure.
method: Fabricated AlCuFe QC coatings by detonation spraying (DS)
Aged the coatings in air at 450�b0C for up to 250 h
Tracked oxidation kinetics, phase evolution, and microhardness
evidence_signal: The coating followed parabolic oxidation kinetics with kp = 1.83 �d7 10^-2 mg^2�b7cm^-4�b7h^-1, while microhardness increased from 491 HV0.2 to 665.2 HV0.2 after 250 h.
watch_item: Performance depends on maintaining the multiphase QC-derived microstructure; excessive Al depletion or service conditions beyond the tested 450�b0C/250 h window may alter oxidation and hardness behavior.
paper_title: High-temperature oxidation kinetics and microstructural evolution of detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings
